question: What 2025-2026 industry reports from Gartner, Forrester, McKinsey, or Clutch compare verified project success rates (completion %, on-time delivery %, satisfaction scores) for landing page/marketing site projects across freelance platforms, traditional agencies, productized services, and no-code tools like Webflow/Framer, including specific numerical benchmarks?

research.question: What 2025-2026 industry reports from Gartner, Forrester, McKinsey, or Clutch compare verified project success rates (completion %, on-time delivery %, satisfaction scores) for landing page/marketing site projects across freelance platforms, traditional agencies, productized services, and no-code tools like Webflow/Framer, including specific numerical benchmarks?

findings.claim: No 2025-2026 industry reports from Gartner, Forrester, McKinsey, or Clutch compare verified project success rates (completion %, on-time delivery %, satisfaction scores) specifically for landing page/marketing site projects across freelance platforms, traditional agencies, productized services, and no-code tools.
findings.reasoning: Multiple targeted searches across analyst firms and Clutch returned general web dev, software dev, and PM stats but no comparative benchmarks for the specified project types or service models; aligns with prior established knowledge gap confirmed in fetched Clutch reports.

findings.claim: Clutch's 2025 web development and web design reports provide general industry stats (e.g., 53% mobile users abandon slow sites, 73% companies rely on design for differentiation) but no project success metrics or cross-model comparisons for landing pages.
findings.reasoning: Directly from fetched Clutch pages; stats focus on trends like AI adoption and mobile optimization, not completion/on-time/satisfaction rates.

findings.claim: PMI/Wellingtone 2025-26 project management stats show general success rates (31% successful, 50% challenged, 19% failed per CHAOS dataset) but not isolated to landing pages or compared across freelance/agency/productized/no-code models.
findings.reasoning: Single source (PM Study Circle summarizing PMI/Wellingtone); broad PM benchmarks without specificity to web/landing page projects or service delivery models.

key_insights: Confirmed data opacity: No verified comparative benchmarks exist for landing page project success across service models in specified reports.
General PM failure rates (19%) and web trends (e.g., mobile abandonment) highlight risks but lack model-specific granularity.
Clutch reports emphasize market growth and AI/mobile trends over outcome metrics.
knowledge_gaps: Specific numerical benchmarks (completion %, on-time %, satisfaction) for landing page projects from Gartner/Forrester/McKinsey/Clutch.
Any cross-comparisons between freelance platforms, agencies, productized services, and no-code tools.
Reasons why these firms avoid publishing such data (strategic opacity inferred but unconfirmed).
research.summary: No 2025-2026 reports from Gartner, Forrester, McKinsey, or Clutch provide the requested comparative project success benchmarks for landing page/marketing site projects across service models. Fetched Clutch reports offer general web dev trends (e.g., mobile optimization stats) and PMI data shows broad PM success rates (31% successful), but nothing specific to landing pages or cross-model comparisons. This reinforces the established transparency gap in freelance/agency outcome data.

strengths: Excellent source verification - all cited URLs were actually fetched
High intellectual honesty in acknowledging data gaps and limitations
Thorough search strategy across multiple targeted queries
Clear distinction between what was found vs. what was sought
Appropriate confidence levels assigned to claims
weaknesses: Limited depth in exploring why this data gap exists
Could have attempted alternative search strategies or proxy metrics
Missing analysis of what the general PM statistics might imply for the specific use case
No exploration of industry reasons for lack of transparency

[CONFIDENCE CHECK] 2 'high' confidence claim(s) lack multi-source backing (accuracy -1.0): 'No 2025-2026 industry reports from Gartner, Forrester, McKinsey, or Clutch compa...' (high confidence, only 1 source(s))
critique.actionable_feedback: Consider expanding the research by: 1) Searching for case studies or academic papers that might contain project success comparisons, 2) Looking for industry surveys from web development communities or platforms themselves, 3) Analyzing the general PM failure rates in context of what they might mean for landing page projects specifically, 4) Investigating why major consulting firms avoid publishing such granular comparative data
